meta.title: CJNG Retaliatory Violence — Zihuatanejo Area, February 22, 2026
meta.subtitle: Incidents following the killing of "El Mencho" in Tapalpa, Jalisco

infoBox.vehicleTypes: Cargo trucks, transit Urvans (Las Dos Costas), private cars, motorcycle
infoBox.coordNote: Coordinates sourced from INEGI, Mapcarta, PueblosAmerica, postal code databases. Some approximate.

incidents.title: 1. Cargo truck burned — Libramiento a Pantla / La Salitrera
incidents.detail: Armed men forced driver of a cargo truck out at gunpoint and set truck on fire. La Salitrera is a hamlet near Pantla, about 10 km NW of Zihuatanejo on the bypass road.

incidents.title: 2. Parked car burned — Colonia Nuevo Amanecer
incidents.detail: Armed men with gasoline set fire to a parked automobile alongside Bulevar Paseo de Zihuatanejo in Colonia Nuevo Amanecer.
incidents.source: Coords: Approx. — Col. Nuevo Amanecer along Blvd. Paseo de Zihuatanejo

incidents.title: 3. Vehicles burned — Colonia La Noria (x2 incidents)
incidents.detail: Compact vehicles set on fire alongside Blvd. Paseo de Zihuatanejo in Col. La Noria. At ~6:00 PM, another automobile burned near the La Noria market.
incidents.source: Coords: Approx. — Col. La Noria, east side of Zihuatanejo

incidents.title: 4. Two 'Las Dos Costas' transit Urvans + private car — Carretera near Lagunillas / San Jerónimo
incidents.detail: Two Urvan-type public transit vans of the 'Las Dos Costas' company + one private automobile burned on the highway near Lagunillas and San Jerónimo.
incidents.source: Coords: Midpoint between INEGI-verified Lagunillas and San Jerónimo La Caliza

incidents.title: 5. ATTEMPTED Urvan burning — Airport route / Chedraui
incidents.detail: Armed men attempted to burn a public transit Urvan in front of the Chedraui supermarket on the road toward the airport. Firefighters responded quickly and PREVENTED full destruction.
incidents.source: Coords: Approx. — Chedraui supermarket on airport road, SE of centro

incidents.title: 6. Car burned — Calle Palmas, Centro (downtown)
incidents.detail: Compact automobile set on fire on Calle Palmas in downtown Zihuatanejo. Massive multi-agency response.
incidents.source: Coords: Calle Palmas, Zihuatanejo Centro — confirmed downtown street

incidents.title: 7a. Vehicle burned — El Embalse
incidents.detail: Vehicle burning reported in Colonia El Embalse. Located ~1 km west of Zihuatanejo centro.
incidents.source: Coords: PueblosAmerica/INEGI — El Embalse, ~1km W of centro, CP 40894

incidents.title: 7b. Vehicle burned — El Coacoyul
incidents.detail: Vehicle burning reported in El Coacoyul, an outlying community ~8 km east of Zihuatanejo centro.

incidents.title: 7c. Vehicle burned — Agua de Correa
incidents.detail: Armed men with gasoline set fire to a vehicle in Agua de Correa, ~2 km east of Zihuatanejo centro.
incidents.source: Coords: PueblosAmerica — Agua de Correa, ~2km E of Zihuatanejo centro

incidents.title: 7d. Vehicle burned — Laguna Las Salinas area
incidents.detail: Vehicle burning reported at Las Salinas. The Laguna Las Salinas is a lagoon in central Zihuatanejo.
incidents.source: Coords: UNAM project data / Hotel Las Salinas — central Zihuatanejo lagoon area

incidents.title: 8. Motorcycle burned — location unspecified
incidents.detail: At least one motorcycle was among the vehicles burned. Exact location within the municipality not specified.

incidents.title: Las Dos Costas Transit Co. — HQ Raided
incidents.detail: Armed men went to the company headquarters, forcibly removed parked transit vehicles, and burned them on the street.

shutdowns.title: Sam's Club / Walmart — Ixtapa
shutdowns.detail: Closed preemptively to avoid being targeted by armed groups.

shutdowns.title: Soriana + all Oxxo stores — Zihuatanejo
shutdowns.detail: Closed preemptively. All Oxxo convenience stores in the area also shuttered.

shutdowns.title: Ixtapa Hotel Zone — Shelter-in-place
shutdowns.detail: Resorts sheltered guests. Occupancy was 86.8% in Ixtapa.

disruptions.title: Lázaro Cárdenas, Michoacán — Port & Transit Disruption
disruptions.detail: C5 Michoacán confirmed traffic disruptions. SAT office closed. DHL reported reduced freight volumes. Trucking capacity tightened as carriers refused to send drivers into western Mexico.
disruptions.source: Sources: C5 Michoacán, CNBC, Azteca Bajío, Excélsior

disruptions.title: Port of Lázaro Cárdenas — Freight Disruption
disruptions.detail: One of Mexico's largest Pacific ports and a key entry point for precursor chemicals from Asia. DHL reported lower-than-normal volumes. Port operations continued at reduced capacity.
